title: Project Report: Todo App Full Stack — Full Stack Web Developer
author: Gagan Pasupuleti
summary: React frontend + Express API + SQLite: create, complete, delete todos with persistence.
source_file: CodeQuest project specification (official docs + industry patterns)

chapters.title: Project Overview
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Project Overview** *(Level: Beginner)*

**Todo App Full Stack** is a beginner-level end-to-end project for Full Stack Web Developer. Duration: 2 weeks. React frontend + Express API + SQLite: create, complete, delete todos with persistence. Tools: React, Node.js, Express, SQLite.

Code Reference:
```text
Objective: REST API for todos
Objective: React UI with forms
Objective: SQLite persistence
Objective: Basic error handling
```
What it shows: Lists measurable outcomes before you start building.

### What it actually is
A project overview defines scope, tools, and success criteria.

### When to use it
At project kickoff.

### Where to use it
Full Stack Web Developer portfolios and CodeQuest project submissions.

### Real use example
A candidate lists this project on a Full Stack Web Developer resume with a demo link.
key_takeaways: A project overview defines scope, tools, and success criteria.
A candidate lists this project on a Full Stack Web Developer resume with a demo link.

chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Phase: API** *(Level: Beginner)*

Express CRUD routes. Complete these tasks: SQLite schema; Validation. Deliverable for this phase: API tested with curl.

Code Reference:
```text
- [ ] SQLite schema
- [ ] Validation
```
What it shows: Checklist format helps track phase completion.

### Where to use it

### Real use example

chapters.title: Final Deliverables and Review
chapters.content:
**Chapter focus: Final Deliverables and Review** *(Level: Beginner)*

Submit all final deliverables: GitHub monorepo; Live frontend; Live API. Skills demonstrated: React, Express, SQL. Prepare a 5-minute walkthrough video and README for CodeQuest teacher marking.

Code Reference:
```text
Deliverable: GitHub monorepo
Deliverable: Live frontend
Deliverable: Live API
```
What it shows: Final checklist ensures nothing is missing at submission.

### What it actually is
Deliverable review confirms end-to-end project completion.

### When to use it
Before deadline and teacher review.

### Where to use it
CodeQuest project gallery and interviews.

### Real use example
A student submits GitHub monorepo and passes the rubric.
